metadata.author: Garrett
metadata.privateContent:
Objective: recover an enterprise monorepo auth migration without leaving half-migrated services or invalid sessions in production.

Procedure: freeze writes, capture a signed migration snapshot, validate token issuer compatibility, restore old verifier configuration, replay only idempotent schema steps, and re-enable traffic behind a kill switch.

Validation: rerun auth benchmark cases, verify session validator invariants, and confirm rollback audit receipts include repo, commit, workflow run, and migration batch.

Expected touched areas: services/auth/rollback.ts, config/auth/issuer-compat.yml, and the ENGI remediation branch validation notes.

metadata.author: Eve
metadata.privateContent:
Contract: session validation lookups must not panic, must preserve index bounds, and must never accept expired or issuer-mismatched entries.

Implementation note: replace unchecked indexing with guarded access, propagate Result and Option values, and prove that cache eviction preserves validator invariants.

Validation steps: cargo test -p auth-validator, cargo creusot prove services/auth/session_validator.rs, and rerun the GitHub Actions auth remediation benchmark.

Expected touched areas: services/auth/session_validator.rs and services/auth/cache.rs.

metadata.author: Avery
metadata.privateContent:
Incident pattern: auth migration changed issuer and audience validation together, which broke older services still pinned to the previous JWKS endpoint.

Recovery pattern: split issuer migration from audience migration, ship an explicit compatibility window, and add a runtime audit that rejects mixed config before rollout.

Operator note: most failed recoveries came from restoring schema before restoring validator config.

conformanceProfiles.active: Profile A — local deterministic V8 prototype
conformanceProfiles.productionIntent: Profile B — GitHub/App and external production boundary

profiles.label: Profile A — local deterministic V8 prototype
Profile B — GitHub/App and external production boundary
profiles.whyNotSwitchable: This repo only demos Profile A deterministically; Profile B depends on external GitHub/App/runtime integrations not faked here.

expectedConstraints: keep remediation branch private until settlement completes
expectedFailureModes: rollback restores schema before verifier compatibility
audit receipts missing repo and commit linkage
needScenarios.expectedTask: Recover a production auth migration with issuer mismatch while preserving session validity and rollback safety.
needScenarios.humanPrompt: Need a private ENGI remediation branch for auth rollback failures observed in the buyer PR.

reasons: Authorized reviewer access is allowed under private review policy.
Authorized reviewers may not trigger settlement.
Buyer principals do not publish bounded public proof metadata directly.
Buyer event authorizes licensed source material to be staged into the private branch.
Delivery-open is blocked in the deterministic local prototype.
Buyer may inspect bounded public proof metadata.
Buyer principal may inspect private remediation delivery.
Buyer principal may authorize the fixed local settlement event.
ENGI proof publisher may derive bounded proof metadata from the private proof surface.
ENGI branch materializer may stage selected source material under .engi/source-material/.
ENGI system principal may inspect bounded proof metadata.
ENGI system principal materializes private artifacts.
ENGI settlement engine executes deterministic journal settlement.
ENGI system principal stages remediation artifacts.
Issuers do not gain read access by default.
Issuers cannot self-settle the buyer event.
Only bounded public proof metadata may be public.
Private remediation material is never public by default.

specVersion: ENGI Spec V8 deterministic local prototype
